div.div: How to parse and read SketchUp (.skp) files programmatically in Python or JavaScript without the official C++ SDK?

there is no complete, production-ready, pure-Python or pure-JS library for modern SKP files without native dependencies, but there are several real paths forward depending on your constraints. if you want a proper architecture then - accept the SKP upload --> run Blender headless in a Docker Lambda container to convert to GLB --> store the GLB --> serve it to the browser for WebGL rendering via Three.js. This sidesteps the closed-format problem entirely and gives you full fidelity.

In IntelliJ, go to Run Configuration then Environment Variables and add: SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=dev Remove -Dspring.profiles.active=dev from VM options.
DevTools replaces the classloader at startup and drops system properties early, but environment variables are unaffected. :))

div.p: Hey! I'm Anshuman 🩷🌻 I build things. All kinds of things. Open source libraries, developer tools, apps, research projects, tutorials, content — if it's worth creating, I'm probably working on it. My work is free and open because I believe great tools should be accessible to everyone. But building and maintaining takes real time and energy — and that's where you come in. When you sponsor me, you're not just supporting one project. You're fueling an entire ecosystem of things being built, shipped, and shared freely with the community. Every contribution — big or small — means I can spend more time creating and less time worrying. Thank you for being here.
